Effective Date, , , provided that:Pub. L. 99–410, title II, § 204100 Stat. 930Aug. 28, 1986“The amendments and repeals made by this Act [see Tables for classification] shall apply with respect to elections taking place after .”December 31, 1987

Sense of Congress Regarding the Importance of Voting, , , provided that:Pub. L. 107–107, div. A, title XVI, § 1601115 Stat. 1274Dec. 28, 2001“(a).—Sense of CongressIt is the sense of Congress that each person who is an administrator of a Federal, State, or local election—“(1)should be aware of the importance of the ability of each uniformed services voter to exercise the right to vote; and“(2)should perform that person’s duties as an election administrator with the intent to ensure that—“(A)each uniformed services voter receives the utmost consideration and cooperation when voting;“(B)each valid ballot cast by such a voter is duly counted; and“(C)all eligible American voters, regardless of race, ethnicity, disability, the language they speak, or the resources of the community in which they live, should have an equal opportunity to cast a vote and to have that vote counted.“(b).—Uniformed Services Voter DefinedIn this section, the term ‘uniformed services voter’ means—“(1)a member of a uniformed service (as defined in , United States Code) in active service;section 101(a)(5) of title 10“(2)a member of the merchant marine (as defined in section 107 of the Uniformed and Overseas Citizens Absentee Voting Act () [now ]); and42 U.S.C. 1973ff–652 U.S.C. 20310“(3)a spouse or dependent of a member referred to in paragraph (1) or (2) who is qualified to vote.”

Electronic Voting Demonstration Project, , , as amended by , , , which established a demonstration project under which absent uniformed services voters were permitted to vote in the November 2002 general election for Federal office through an electronic voting system, was repealed by , , .Pub. L. 107–107, div. A, title XVI, § 1604115 Stat. 1277Pub. L. 108–375, div. A, title V, § 567118 Stat. 1919Pub. L. 113–291, div. A, title V, § 593128 Stat. 3395Dec. 28, 2001Oct. 28, 2004Dec. 19, 2014

Governors’ Reports on Implementation of Recommendations for Changes in State Law Made Under Federal Voting Assistance Program, , , required the chief executive authority of a State to provide an implementation status report to the Secretary of Defense, acting as the Presidential designee, within 90 days of receiving a uniformed services voting assistance legislative recommendation from the Secretary of Defense and was applicable to any uniformed services voting assistance legislative recommendation transmitted to a state during the three-year period beginning on .Pub. L. 107–107, div. A, title XVI, § 1605115 Stat. 1277Dec. 28, 2001Dec. 28, 2001

Ex. Ord. No. 12642. Designation of Secretary of Defense as Presidential DesigneeEx. Ord. No. 12642, , 53 F.R. 21975, provided:June 8, 1988By virtue of the authority vested in me as President by the Constitution and laws of the United States of America, including section 101(a) of the Uniformed and Overseas Citizens Absentee Voting Act () (“the Act”) [], it is hereby ordered as follows:Public Law 99–41052 U.S.C. 20301(a)1. The Secretary of Defense is hereby designated as the “Presidential designee” under Title I of the Act [ et seq.].Section52 U.S.C. 20301. 2. In order to effectuate the purposes of the Act, the Secretary of Defense is hereby authorized to delegate any or all of the functions, responsibilities, powers, authority, or discretion devolving upon him in consequence of this Order to any person or persons within the Department of Defense.SecRonald Reagan.

children.heading: Presidential designee
children.label: a Presidential designee
children.text: The President shall designate the head of an executive department to have primary responsibility for Federal functions under this chapter.
consult State and local election officials in carrying out this chapter, and ensure that such officials are aware of the requirements of this Act;

prescribe an official post card form, containing both an absentee voter registration application and an absentee ballot application, for use by the States as required under ;section 20302(a)(4) of this title
carry out with respect to the Federal write-in absentee ballot for absent uniformed services voters and overseas voters in general elections for Federal office;section 20303 of this title
prescribe a suggested design for absentee ballot mailing envelopes;

compile and distribute (A) descriptive material on State absentee registration and voting procedures, and (B) to the extent practicable, facts relating to specific elections, including dates, offices involved, and the text of ballot questions;

not later than the end of each year after a Presidential election year, transmit to the President and the Congress a report on the effectiveness of assistance under this chapter, including a statistical analysis of uniformed services voter participation, a separate statistical analysis of overseas nonmilitary participation, and a description of State-Federal cooperation;

prescribe a standard oath for use with any document under this chapter affirming that a material misstatement of fact in the completion of such a document may constitute grounds for a conviction for perjury;
carry out with respect to the collection and delivery of marked absentee ballots of absent overseas uniformed services voters in elections for Federal office;section 20304 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: to ensure that absent uniformed services voters who cast absentee ballots at locations or facilities under the jurisdiction of the Presidential designee are able to do so in a private and independent manner; and

children.label: B
children.text: to protect the privacy of the contents of absentee ballots cast by absentee uniformed services voters and overseas voters while such ballots are in the possession or control of the Presidential designee;
carry out with respect to Federal Voting Assistance Program Improvements; andsection 20305 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: for States to report data on the number of absentee ballots transmitted and received under and such other data as the Presidential designee determines appropriate; andsection 20302(c) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: for the Presidential designee to store the data reported.

children.heading: Duties of other Federal officials
children.label: c Duties of other Federal officials

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The head of each Government department, agency, or other entity shall, upon request of the Presidential designee, distribute balloting materials and otherwise cooperate in carrying out this chapter.

children.heading: Administrator of General Services
children.label: 2 Administrator of General Services
children.text: As directed by the Presidential designee, the Administrator of General Services shall furnish official post card forms (prescribed under subsection (b)) and Federal write-in absentee ballots (prescribed under ).section 20303 of this title

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations for carrying out Federal Voting Assistance Program Improvements
children.label: d Authorization of appropriations for carrying out Federal Voting Assistance Program Improvements
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated to the Presidential designee such sums as are necessary for purposes of carrying out subsection (b)(10).
